proposals.title: Fix health check logic inconsistency in recordError
proposals.rationale: In `recordSuccess`, the health check allows `lastErrorTime === 0` (no errors yet) to be healthy, but `recordError` always sets `lastErrorTime = Date.now()` before the check, so the `> 30000` condition is always false immediately after an error. More critically, `recordError` omits the `lastErrorTime === 0` fallback that `recordSuccess` has, creating an inconsistency. The real bug is that a single error immediately marks a provider unhealthy regardless of error rate, since `Date.now() - m.lastErrorTime` will be ~0. The health check should primarily rely on error rate, with a cooldown only for recently-errored providers.

proposals.title: Improve readability of fallbackSummary method
proposals.rationale: The fallbackSummary method uses a complex regex to extract file paths, which is hard to read and maintain. Replacing it with a simpler approach using JSON.parse improves clarity and reduces the risk of regex errors.

proposals.title: Simplify version string to single source of truth
proposals.rationale: The manifest hardcodes '6.15.0' in generateManifest() while the system prompt addendum says 'v6.13', creating inconsistency. Extracting the version to a constant ensures alignment and easier maintenance.
proposals.diff:
===================================================================
--- a/manifest.ts
+++ b/manifest.ts
@@ -361,7 +361,7 @@
   });
 
   return {
-    version: "6.15.0",
+    version: APP_VERSION,
     identity: "Andromeda",
     generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
     sections: allSections,

proposals.title: Improve readability of diversity scoring logic
proposals.rationale: The diversity scoring logic is currently a single monolithic function with inline calculations, making it hard to follow and maintain. Extracting the score calculation into a helper function and using clearer variable names improves readability without changing behavior.

proposals.title: Extract eviction logic into a dedicated function
proposals.rationale: The publish function contains inline eviction logic that mixes concerns and reduces readability. Extracting it into a separate `evictOldEntries` function clarifies the publish flow and makes the eviction policy easier to test and modify independently.
proposals.proposedSnippet:
evictOldEntries();

  // Notify subscribers

proposals.title: Improve readability by consolidating workspace directory logic
proposals.rationale: The getWorkspaceDir function duplicates the logic for resolving the server directory and creating the workspace, which is already handled in getServerDir. By reusing getServerDir, the code becomes more readable and maintainable, reducing redundancy.

proposals.title: Add JSDoc re-export comments for clarity
proposals.rationale: Adding JSDoc comments to each re-export line documents the purpose of each sub-module directly in the barrel file, improving readability for developers scanning the exports. This makes it immediately clear what each module provides without needing to open separate files.
proposals.diff:
===================================================================
--- a/ai.ts
+++ b/ai.ts
@@ -13,7 +13,11 @@
  * to work without modification.
  */
 
+/** Token counting and model/provider helpers */
 export * from "./aiTokens.js";
+/** System prompt builders */
 export * from "./aiPrompts.js";
+/** Core SSE streaming engine and public streaming API */
 export * from "./aiStreaming.js";
+/** Agent planning, Claude Code capabilities, todo system */
 export * from "./aiPlanning.js";
proposals.originalContent:
/**
 * ai.ts — v6.25 (barrel export)
 *
 * This file is now a thin barrel that re-exports everything from the focused
 * sub-modules created in the v6.25 god-module split:
 *
 *   aiTokens.ts    — Token counting and model/provider helpers
 *   aiPrompts.ts   — System prompt builders
 *   aiStreaming.ts — Core SSE streaming engine and public streaming API
 *   aiPlanning.ts  — Agent planning, Claude Code capabilities, todo system
 *
 * All existing import paths (e.g. `import { streamChat } from "./ai"`) continue
 * to work without modification.
 */

export * from "./aiTokens.js";
export * from "./aiPrompts.js";
export * from "./aiStreaming.js";
export * from "./aiPlanning.js";

proposals.title: Extract provider config into separate module
proposals.rationale: The file mixes static provider definitions with runtime logic, making it hard to maintain and test. Extracting DEFAULT_PROVIDERS into a dedicated config file improves separation of concerns and allows providers to be updated independently.
proposals.proposedSnippet:
// DEFAULT_PROVIDERS moved to ./providers.ts
import { DEFAULT_PROVIDERS } from "./providers.js";

proposals.title: Extract inline routes into separate modules
proposals.rationale: The file contains over 20 inline route handlers, making it difficult to maintain and test. Extracting each route group into dedicated modules (e.g., searchRoutes, chatRoutes, workspaceRoutes) would improve modularity, testability, and readability.

proposals.title: Remove unused DEEPSEEK_API_KEY check
proposals.rationale: The runTeamAgent function checks for process.env.DEEPSEEK_API_KEY but the runAgent function no longer uses it, having switched to an active provider via llmProvider. Removing this check eliminates a dead code path and potential confusion about required environment variables.
proposals.proposedSnippet:
// API key is now managed by the LLM provider; no need to check here.
  const apiKey = "";

proposals.title: Improve readability of diff generator logic
proposals.rationale: The current diff generation algorithm is hard to follow due to its inline hunk management and unclear variable names. Refactoring it into a clearer structure with helper functions and descriptive names makes the code easier to understand and maintain.

proposals.title: Extract duplicate file preloading logic into a helper method
proposals.rationale: The same file preloading logic appears in three places (run() pre-load, strike 3-4 injection, and the pre-load fallback), making the code harder to maintain and increasing the risk of inconsistencies. Extracting it into a private method improves readability by reducing duplication and clarifying intent.
proposals.proposedSnippet: const preloadedFiles = await this.preloadServerFiles(["llmProvider.ts", "reactEngine.ts", "truncationDetector.ts"]);

proposals.title: Extract provider chain builder to separate function
proposals.rationale: The inline buildProviderFallbackChain function inside analyzeAndPropose is deeply nested and uses closures over variables that are imported later, making the code hard to follow. Extracting it to a top-level function with explicit parameters improves readability and testability.

proposals.title: Improve readability of extractFactualClaims function
proposals.rationale: The function uses multiple regex patterns with complex comments that are hard to read. Extracting patterns into named constants with descriptive names makes the code self-documenting and easier to maintain.
proposals._failReason: Permanently rejected: repeated syntax failures (v9.8.5 cleanup)

proposals.title: Extract provider fallback chain builder to separate function
proposals.rationale: The buildProviderFallbackChain function is defined inline inside analyzeAndPropose, making the function longer and harder to read. Extracting it to a module-level function improves readability and testability.

proposals.title: Remove redundant barrel export comment
proposals.rationale: The JSDoc comment is overly verbose and repeats information that is obvious from the export statements. Removing it improves readability by reducing noise and keeping the file concise.
proposals.diff:
===================================================================
--- a/ai.ts
+++ b/ai.ts
@@ -1,17 +1,4 @@

- *   aiTokens.ts    — Token counting and model/provider helpers
- *   aiPrompts.ts   — System prompt builders
- *   aiStreaming.ts — Core SSE streaming engine and public streaming API
- *   aiPlanning.ts  — Agent planning, Claude Code capabilities, todo system
- *
- * All existing import paths (e.g. `import { streamChat } from "./ai"`) continue
- * to work without modification.

+// Barrel re-export for backward compatibility
 
 export * from "./aiTokens.js";
 export * from "./aiPrompts.js";

/**
 * ai.ts — v6.25 (barrel export)
 *
 * This file is now a thin barrel that re-exports everything from the focused
 * sub-modules created in the v6.25 god-module split:
 *
 *   aiTokens.ts    — Token counting and model/provider helpers
 *   aiPrompts.ts   — System prompt builders

export * from "./aiPrompts.js";

proposals.title: Improve fallbackSummary method readability
proposals.rationale: The fallbackSummary method uses a complex loop with multiple conditions that makes it hard to follow. Extracting the logic into smaller helper functions and using early returns improves readability and maintainability.

proposals.title: Fix version string inconsistency in manifest
proposals.rationale: The manifest version is hardcoded as '6.15.0' while the system prompt addendum repeatedly states 'v6.13', causing confusion. Aligning both to a single source of truth improves readability and maintainability.

   return {

     identity: "Andromeda",
     generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
     sections: allSections,

proposals.title: Improve readability of SEED_MEMORIES array formatting
proposals.rationale: The SEED_MEMORIES array has inconsistent indentation and a stray blank line in the middle of a string, making it harder to read and maintain. Fixing the formatting improves code clarity without changing behavior.
proposals.diff:
===================================================================
--- a/memory.ts
+++ b/memory.ts
@@ -537,8 +537,6 @@
   },
   {
     content: "Truncation fix (v5.68+): streaming max_tokens raised from 4096 to 32768. Syntax-aware truncation detection added in v5.68 — checks unclosed code fences, unbalanced braces, truncated JSON. Continuation prompt now includes last 200 chars of cut-off content for precise resumption.",
-
-
     type: "error",
     tags: ["truncation", "streaming", "max_tokens", "fix"],
   },

proposals.title: Extract magic numbers into named constants
proposals.rationale: The diversity scoring function uses hardcoded magic numbers (e.g., 50, 30, 10, 20, 5) that obscure the logic's intent. Extracting these into well-named constants improves readability and makes the scoring formula self-documenting.

proposals.title: Extract SSRF protection into a separate module
proposals.rationale: The SSRF protection logic (PRIVATE_IP_PATTERNS, BLOCKED_HOSTNAMES, isPrivateAddress) is duplicated across browseUrl and browserNavigate, making the code harder to maintain and increasing the risk of inconsistencies. Extracting it into a dedicated module improves readability by centralizing the security logic and reducing clutter in the browser functions.
proposals.proposedSnippet: import { isPrivateAddress } from "./ssrfGuard";

proposals.title: Extract duplicate file preloading logic into helper method
proposals.rationale: The file preloading logic for self-modification tasks appears in three places (initial preload, fake tool call escalation, and the zero-tool-results guard), each with nearly identical code. Extracting it into a private method reduces duplication, improves maintainability, and ensures consistent behavior across all preload sites.
proposals.proposedSnippet: const preloadedFiles = await this.preloadServerFiles(["llmProvider.ts", "reactEngine.ts", "truncationDetector.ts"]);

proposals.title: Extract provider query into reusable helper
proposals.rationale: The provider query functions share identical error handling and evaluation logic, which is duplicated and obscures the core flow. Extracting a helper reduces duplication and makes the main checkSelfConsistency function more readable.

proposals.title: Refactor diff generator for readability
proposals.rationale: The current diff generator uses a confusing LCS-based algorithm with manual hunk flushing, making it hard to understand and maintain. Replacing it with a simpler line-by-line comparison using a standard diff library improves clarity and reduces cognitive load.

proposals.title: Remove unused DEEPSEEK_API_KEY check and apiKey parameter
proposals.rationale: The runTeamAgent function checks for DEEPSEEK_API_KEY and passes it to runAgent, but runAgent no longer uses the apiKey parameter (it imports simpleChatCompletion from llmProvider). Removing this dead code simplifies the function and eliminates a misleading environment variable requirement.
proposals.proposedSnippet: // No API key needed; llmProvider handles authentication internally.

proposals.title: Extract remaining inline routes into separate modules
proposals.rationale: The file still contains many inline route handlers (e.g., /api/search/stream, /api/chat/stream) that could be extracted into dedicated route modules for better maintainability and testability. This would reduce the file size and follow the pattern already established with memoryRoutes, selfRoutes, etc.

proposals.title: Fix truncated seed memory entry
proposals.rationale: The second entry in SEED_MEMORIES has its content string split across two lines with a blank line in between, which will cause a syntax error or malformed string. Joining the lines fixes the readability and correctness of the seed data.

-
-
     type: "error",
     tags: ["truncation", "streaming", "max_tokens", "fix"],
   },

proposals.title: Extract version constant to single source of truth
proposals.rationale: The version string '6.15.0' is hardcoded in generateManifest() while the system prompt addendum references '6.13' and package.json uses '6.13.0'. Extracting a single VERSION constant eliminates inconsistency and makes future version bumps a one-line change.
proposals.diff:

   });
 
   return {
-    version: "6.15.0",
+    version: VERSION,
     identity: "Andromeda",
     generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
     sections: allSections,

proposals.title: Simplify diff generator logic for readability
proposals.rationale: The current diff generator uses a complex LCS-based approach that is hard to follow. Replacing it with a simpler Myers-like algorithm or a more straightforward line-by-line comparison improves readability and maintainability.

proposals.title: Extract SSRF and readability logic into separate modules
proposals.rationale: The file is over 400 lines and mixes URL validation, SSRF protection, readability extraction, and session management. Extracting SSRF and readability into dedicated modules improves readability and maintainability by separating concerns.
proposals.proposedSnippet: import { isPrivateAddress } from "./ssrf";

proposals.title: Fix unbalanced brackets in extractFactualClaims refactor
proposals.rationale: The previous proposal had two extra closing brackets in the extractDates function's regex. Removed them to fix syntax error.
proposals._failReason: Permanently rejected: repeated syntax failures (v9.8.5 cleanup)

proposals.title: Consolidate workspace directory logic into single function
proposals.rationale: The workspace directory resolution is duplicated across getWorkspaceDir and safeWorkspacePath, making the code harder to maintain. Consolidating the logic into a single getWorkspaceDir function and removing the redundant safeWorkspacePath improves readability and reduces the risk of inconsistencies.

proposals.title: Remove redundant env var check in selectProvider
proposals.rationale: The selectProvider function checks process.env[provider.apiKeyEnv] again after initProviders already sets provider.enabled based on the same env var. This duplicate check is unnecessary and can cause subtle bugs if the env var changes at runtime, while the provider.enabled flag remains consistent with the init-time state.
